title: Static maps, dynamic threats: re-evaluating U.S. wildfire risk with spatiotemporal clustering
content: This study provides a national-scale comparison of purely spatial and spatiotemporal clustering methods to identify wildfire (WF) hotspots across the contiguous United States (CONUS) from 2014 to 2024.
snippet: This study provides a national-scale comparison of purely spatial and spatiotemporal clustering methods to identify wildfire (WF) hotspots across the contiguous United States (CONUS) from 2014 to 2024

title: Responding to floods in Pakistan with adaptive social protection: How to keep an inundated country afloat
content: This note aims to identify ways to work with the existing social protection system to build a well-functioning Adaptive Social Protection System, which would build pre-shock resilience and provide rapid and adequate response in times of crises.
snippet: This note aims to identify ways to work with the existing social protection system to build a well-functioning Adaptive Social Protection System, which would build pre-shock resilience and provide rap

title: Ending gender-based violence in disaster risks and climate resilience by 2030
content: This issue of Southasiadisasters.net examines how disasters, displacement, extreme heat, migration, water scarcity, recovery processes, and climate adaptation can intensify violence against women and girls when protection systems are weak or absent.
snippet: This issue of Southasiadisasters.net examines how disasters, displacement, extreme heat, migration, water scarcity, recovery processes, and climate adaptation can intensify violence against women and

title: UNFCCC call for input from ISC members – mapping progress in filling climate change research gaps
content: Since 2005 scientists & policymakers have identified important gaps in climate research in their deliberations under the SBSTA. The UNFCCC has called for input from ISC Members and the scientific community to highlight progress made in filling these gaps
snippet: Since 2005 scientists & policymakers have identified important gaps in climate research in their deliberations under the SBSTA. The UNFCCC has called for input from ISC Members and the scientific

title: Sinking land drives coastal flood risk on densely populated Java Island
content: A study published in Science Advances reveals that sinking land — not just rising oceans alone — will be the main cause of future coastal flooding along Indonesia’s densely populated Java Island, putting millions at risk sooner than expected.
snippet: A study published in Science Advances reveals that sinking land — not just rising oceans alone — will be the main cause of future coastal flooding along Indonesia’s densely populated Java Island, putt

title: Confronting climate migration in drought-hit East Africa
content: Local solutions are needed to address migration and boost resilience, agreed researchers and policymakers at a conference in Ethiopia
snippet: Local solutions are needed to address migration and boost resilience, agreed researchers and policymakers at a conference in Ethiopia

title: Landscape of climate finance in Ethiopia
content: Ethiopia’s climate policy framework is increasingly investment-oriented, moving from ambition to action.
snippet: Ethiopia’s climate policy framework is increasingly investment-oriented, moving from ambition to action.

title: From forecast to action: using flood impact storylines to optimize mobile levee deployment and emergency response strategies
content: This study shows how flood impact storylines can help emergency managers decide where and when to deploy mobile levees, reducing damage and buying critical time for evacuation during extreme flood events.
snippet: This study shows how flood impact storylines can help emergency managers decide where and when to deploy mobile levees, reducing damage and buying critical time for evacuation during extreme flood eve

title: The Global Risk Modelling Alliance’s (GRMA) Call for proposals for Malawi is now open.
content: The GRMA aims to fill critical model and data gaps and enhance (sub-)sovereign climate and disaster risk assessment capabilities. This Call for Proposals (CfP) reflects the gaps and needs identified and co-defined together with country partners.
snippet: The GRMA aims to fill critical model and data gaps and enhance (sub-)sovereign climate and disaster risk assessment capabilities. This Call for Proposals (CfP) reflects the gaps and needs identified a

title: State consultant in Gujarat: INSPIRE India
content: The State Consultant in Gujarat will be responsible for coordinating and implementing state-level activities under the INSPIRE India program.
snippet: The State Consultant in Gujarat will be responsible for coordinating and implementing state-level activities under the INSPIRE India program.

title: Smokey knows: President Trump’s Forest service restructuring is bad news
content: The expansive restructuring of the agency is irreversibly destructive to the federal scientific enterprise and leaves the nation to face growing climate threats with fewer experts predicting and managing wildfires.
snippet: The expansive restructuring of the agency is irreversibly destructive to the federal scientific enterprise and leaves the nation to face growing climate threats with fewer experts predicting and manag

title: As Indian cities struggle to plan for heat, the most vulnerable suffer
content: Adaptation to heat from climate change, which drives temperatures higher, requires rethinking how cities are planned and developed, say experts in India and worldwide.
snippet: Adaptation to heat from climate change, which drives temperatures higher, requires rethinking how cities are planned and developed, say experts in India and worldwide.

title: Cities battle urban heat: Green solutions for a warming climate
content: UHIs develop when buildings, roads, and other infrastructure made of materials such as steel, stone, and concrete absorb heat from the sun during the day and then release it back into the atmosphere in the afternoon and evening.
snippet: UHIs develop when buildings, roads, and other infrastructure made of materials such as steel, stone, and concrete absorb heat from the sun during the day and then release it back into the atmosphere i

title: Research shows how forests can prevent floods of all sizes
content: Forests play a crucial and often underestimated role in reducing flood risk of all magnitudes, highlighting the need to integrate nature-based solutions with traditional infrastructure for more effective climate adaptation.
snippet: Forests play a crucial and often underestimated role in reducing flood risk of all magnitudes, highlighting the need to integrate nature-based solutions with traditional infrastructure for more effect

title: Preparing for climate extremes means we must work with uncertainty
content: Climate change is expected to increase the frequency and intensity of extreme weather events, which are creating significant uncertainties for local communities as well as private and public agencies across the world.
snippet: Climate change is expected to increase the frequency and intensity of extreme weather events, which are creating significant uncertainties for local communities as well as private and public agencies

title: Risk to resilience: A socio-ecological perspective on the Indians Sundarbans
content: This book examines how socio-ecological factors shape vulnerability and resilience in the Indian Sundarbans, highlighting community-based and ecosystem-based strategies to address multi-hazard risks.
snippet: This book examines how socio-ecological factors shape vulnerability and resilience in the Indian Sundarbans, highlighting community-based and ecosystem-based strategies to address multi-hazard risks.

title: Public financial management for disaster response and adaptive social protection in the Sahel
content: This report examines how governments across Burkina Faso, Chad, Mali, Mauritania and Senegal can better plan and budget for disasters before they strike, setting out practical recommendations for building more responsive social protection systems.
snippet: This report examines how governments across Burkina Faso, Chad, Mali, Mauritania and Senegal can better plan and budget for disasters before they strike, setting out practical recommendations for buil

title: Disasters trigger 69% surge in public protests across Latin America, new research finds
content: Study covering five major economies finds smaller communities face the longest economic recoveries, with damage persisting for up to four months.
snippet: Study covering five major economies finds smaller communities face the longest economic recoveries, with damage persisting for up to four months.

title: Human-induced climate change intensifies spatially compounding fire weather extremes across European countries
content: Study shows how climate change is increasing simultaneous fire weather extremes across Europe, raising risks of large-scale wildfires and strained response systems.
snippet: Study shows how climate change is increasing simultaneous fire weather extremes across Europe, raising risks of large-scale wildfires and strained response systems.

title: Transdisciplinary approaches to urban flood risk and green infrastructure: Lessons from Asahata Reservoir in Shizuoka, Japan
content: Case study of urban flood risk management in Japan showing how green infrastructure and collaborative governance enhance resilience and social co-benefits.
snippet: Case study of urban flood risk management in Japan showing how green infrastructure and collaborative governance enhance resilience and social co-benefits.

title: Innovative approaches to accessing and scaling all sources of adaptation finance in SIDS and LDCs: a compendium of promising case studies
content: Compendium of case studies showcasing innovative approaches to scaling adaptation finance and improving access for climate-vulnerable countries.
snippet: Compendium of case studies showcasing innovative approaches to scaling adaptation finance and improving access for climate-vulnerable countries.

title: The use of cohesion policy in disaster response and recovery
content: Study analysing how EU Cohesion Policy supports disaster risk reduction, recovery and resilience, with recommendations to strengthen build back better approaches.
snippet: Study analysing how EU Cohesion Policy supports disaster risk reduction, recovery and resilience, with recommendations to strengthen build back better approaches.

title: Children displaced in a changing climate: Using historical disaster impact data to understand and map child displacement risk for action and investment
content: This case study demonstrates how disaster losses and damage data can be applied to understand child displacement risk and inform forward-looking planning.
snippet: This case study demonstrates how disaster losses and damage data can be applied to understand child displacement risk and inform forward-looking planning.

title: The 18th Aceh International Workshop and Expo on Sustainable Disaster Recovery
content: The 18th Aceh International Workshop and Expo on Sustainable Disaster Recovery (AIWEST-DR 2026) is bringing together researchers, practitioners, and innovators to share ideas, real solutions, and future-ready strategies for sustainable recovery.
snippet: The 18th Aceh International Workshop and Expo on Sustainable Disaster Recovery (AIWEST-DR 2026) is bringing together researchers, practitioners, and innovators to share ideas, real solutions, and futu

title: Somalia CPS Webinar Series: Building Resilience Through Integrated Climate, Peace and Security Approaches
content: The Somalia Climate, Peace and Security (CPS) series serves as a strategic platform to increase momentum around the climate dialogue in Somalia.
snippet: The Somalia Climate, Peace and Security (CPS) series serves as a strategic platform to increase momentum around the climate dialogue in Somalia.

title: COPE Heatwaves
content: Travel to the city of Paris to discover how the city implements their ‘climate action plan’ to help deal with heatwaves.
snippet: Travel to the city of Paris to discover how the city implements their ‘climate action plan’ to help deal with heatwaves.

title: COPE Floods
content: Join COPE on their Floods mission to Kelantan State, Malaysia, where they put their disaster risk reduction skills into action.
snippet: Join COPE on their Floods mission to Kelantan State, Malaysia, where they put their disaster risk reduction skills into action.

title: COPE Climate Change
content: Join the COPE Squad on their journey to the United Nations Climate Conference, where they meet climate scientists and climate champions to explore key environmental challenges, and discover the power of everyday actions.
snippet: Join the COPE Squad on their journey to the United Nations Climate Conference, where they meet climate scientists and climate champions to explore key environmental challenges, and discover the power

title: Much of humanity may face hot-dry extremes five times more often by end-century
content: The increase may hit nearly 30% of the global population with extreme events more dangerous than heat or drought alone, especially in low-income tropical nations.
snippet: The increase may hit nearly 30% of the global population with extreme events more dangerous than heat or drought alone, especially in low-income tropical nations.

title: Anthropogenic climate change amplifies autumn heatwave risks for children during school reopening
content: In many countries across the Northern Hemisphere, school reopens in early autumn — a time that is supposed to offer a welcome break from the intense summer heat.
snippet: In many countries across the Northern Hemisphere, school reopens in early autumn — a time that is supposed to offer a welcome break from the intense summer heat.

title: How aligning financial strategies with climate finance can drive resilient growth
content: Financial inclusion and countries’ climate objectives are deeply interconnected.
snippet: Financial inclusion and countries’ climate objectives are deeply interconnected.

title: Taming the seismicity tsunami with a scalable Bayesian framework
content: By combining the power of artificial intelligence with advanced physics simulations, a new framework called “SPIDER” allows us to map seismic activity with unprecedented clarity.
snippet: By combining the power of artificial intelligence with advanced physics simulations, a new framework called “SPIDER” allows us to map seismic activity with unprecedented clarity.

title: Integrating working animals into disaster risk management: insights from six countries
content: This study explores how working animals, such as horses, donkeys, and mules, are insufficiently integrated into disaster risk management (DRM) frameworks across Ethiopia, India, Kenya, Nicaragua, Pakistan, and Senegal.
snippet: This study explores how working animals, such as horses, donkeys, and mules, are insufficiently integrated into disaster risk management (DRM) frameworks across Ethiopia, India, Kenya, Nicaragua, Paki

title: Integrated Risk Management Associates LLC (IRMA)
content: IRMA is an international consulting company serving clients who promote equitable, risk-informed development and humanitarian action.
snippet: IRMA is an international consulting company serving clients who promote equitable, risk-informed development and humanitarian action.

title: Who bears the burden of climate inaction?
content: This paper examines several major vectors through which climate change affects US households, including cost increases associated with home insurance claims and increased cooling, as well as sources of increased mortality.
snippet: This paper examines several major vectors through which climate change affects US households, including cost increases associated with home insurance claims and increased cooling, as well as sources o

title: Nature-based solutions in mountains: assessing the potential of nature-based solutions for disaster risk reduction in Central Asia and South Caucasus
content: This study examines climate vulnerability and the potential of nature-based solutions (NBS) across six countries in Central Asia and the South Caucasus: Armenia, Georgia, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyz Republic, Tajikistan, and Uzbekistan.

title: Advance report on forest fires in Europe, Middle East and North Africa 2025
content: This report contains the annual summary of the wildfire season of 2025 on the basis of data from the European Forest Fire Information System (EFFIS).
snippet: This report contains the annual summary of the wildfire season of 2025 on the basis of data from the European Forest Fire Information System (EFFIS).

title: Fiji develops digital tracker to monitor disaster preparedness across government
content: Fiji has completed training officials from roughly 30 ministries and agencies to use a new online system that, for the first time, will let the government track disaster risk reduction work across every sector from a single platform.
snippet: Fiji has completed training officials from roughly 30 ministries and agencies to use a new online system that, for the first time, will let the government track disaster risk reduction work across eve

title: Research shows 41 US states are getting warmer, all in slightly different ways
content: Different regions of the United States are experiencing different patterns of warming climate, requiring region-specific adaptation, according to a study published in PLOS Climate.
snippet: Different regions of the United States are experiencing different patterns of warming climate, requiring region-specific adaptation, according to a study published in PLOS Climate.

title: Modeling mangroves' capacity to protect coastal communities
content: Mangrove forests are natural wonders that protect coastal areas, particularly in tropical and subtropical regions. They are able to dissipate wave energy and limit flooding which can even mitigate tsunamis and coastal inundations during tropical cyclones.
